title: centromadia pungens subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Centromadia pungens subsp. septentrionalis, also known as Madroña or Yerba Buena, is a plant native to the coastal sage scrub region of California and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, thanks to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. Herbal preparations of Madroña include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever reduction and skin health promotion.
